metadata.title: Open Public Infrastructure
metadata.description: A platform where citizens see the infrastructure projects near them, share their views, and report risks. Transparency built into the core.

appMetadata.title: Infrastructure Projects — App
appMetadata.description: See the infrastructure projects near you, give your feedback, and report risks.

home.purposeBody1: The economics of innovation is the economics of systems: a good system measures its results, publishes them, and learns from them. But public projects rarely start by defining the problem or publishing their goal — and without a yardstick to judge them by, they become an «open loop» that repeats the same mistakes, perfectly legally.
home.purposeBody2: This platform closes that loop: it makes visible what is being built, lets citizens have their say while it still matters, and gathers risks and feedback in one place — instead of an FOI form, a wait, and hope.
home.tryBody: See how public infrastructure can look when it's open by default: active and completed projects, citizen feedback, and risk reports — all in one place.
home.aboutBody: I'm Daniel Falzon, a software engineer with ten years' experience building systems. This platform is part of a series of writings on how Malta — small enough to be run well — can build its things with discipline, transparency and care.

principles.label: The Principles
principles.userFirstBody: Every system exists for the people who use it. A good service starts from people and works backwards, not from the department's internal logic.
principles.transparencyBody: A system you can't see into is a system you can't trust. When how a decision was made is visible automatically, anyone can check it.
principles.bestPeopleBody: Competence isn't a luxury in public works; it's the difference between something that serves a generation and something that falls apart again within a few years.
principles.growTalentBody: When the public sector invests in capacity, it strengthens the companies, engineers and skills we have at home instead of importing them forever.

contact.heading: Contact Me
contact.intro: Want to talk about this project, collaborate, or comment on one of my blog posts? Don't hesitate to get in touch!
